agent.root_goal: Produce a bilingual (EN/KR) developer style guide for 'Cheonma', an internal payments platform, in two independently-owned parts. Part A (platform standards team): first survey the naming actually used by Cheonma's three legacy services (ledger, checkout, merchant-portal), recording every legacy identifier, endpoint name, and error code in use; then decide and write the canonical terminology standard (endpoint nouns, error-code vocabulary, casing rules). The published standard lists ONLY the new canonical terms, not the legacy ones. Part B (developer relations team): write the worked integration tutorial with runnable code samples, covering four sections in both languages: the checkout flow, the refund flow, error handling, and migration notes for teams upgrading from the legacy services - the migration notes must map each legacy identifier to its canonical replacement, old -> new. Company handbook rule, applying to the whole guide: documentation ships only if every identifier, endpoint name, and error code appearing anywhere in the guide matches the published canonical standard exactly; term drift between parts is a shipping defect.

action.reasoning: The root task requires producing a bilingual developer style guide split into two parts. Part A (survey and canonical standard) must complete before Part B (tutorial and migration notes) can be written, because Part B depends on the canonical terms and the legacy-to-canonical mapping. I will spawn two subtasks: root.1 for Part A and root.2 for Part B. root.2 will be gated on the completion of root.1 to ensure term consistency. I will take a self_role to perform a final consistency review, ensuring no term drift between the two parts, which is a shipping defect per the handbook rule.

subtasks.goal: Survey legacy naming across ledger, checkout, and merchant-portal services, then define the canonical terminology standard.
subtasks.capsule: This subtask acts as the platform standards team. It must survey the three legacy services (ledger, checkout, merchant-portal) to record every legacy identifier, endpoint name, and error code. Based on this survey, it decides and writes the canonical terminology standard covering endpoint nouns, error-code vocabulary, and casing rules. The published standard lists ONLY the new canonical terms. It also produces a private mapping of legacy-to-canonical terms for use by Part B.

outputs.description: Comprehensive record of all legacy identifiers, endpoint names, and error codes from ledger, checkout, and merchant-portal services.
The published canonical terminology standard listing only new canonical terms for endpoints, error codes, and casing rules.
Internal mapping of every legacy identifier to its canonical replacement, used by Part B for migration notes.

subtasks.goal: Write the bilingual (EN/KR) integration tutorial with runnable code samples and migration notes.
subtasks.capsule: This subtask acts as the developer relations team. It writes the worked integration tutorial covering four sections: checkout flow, refund flow, error handling, and migration notes. All content must be in both English and Korean. The migration notes must map each legacy identifier to its canonical replacement. Crucially, every identifier, endpoint name, and error code used in this tutorial must match the canonical standard produced by root.1 exactly. Term drift is a shipping defect.

outputs.description: Bilingual tutorial section for the checkout flow with runnable code samples.
Bilingual tutorial section for the refund flow with runnable code samples.
Bilingual tutorial section for error handling with runnable code samples.
Bilingual migration notes mapping legacy identifiers to canonical replacements.

self_role.goal: Perform final consistency review to ensure no term drift between Part A and Part B.

outputs.description: Report verifying that all identifiers in Part B match the canonical standard from Part A exactly.
